preamble: The shift from a diphthong [au] to a high back nasalized vowel [ũ] involves both nasalization and vowel raising, often occurring as a result of vowel harmony or nasal assimilation.

examples.note: Development where a nasal environment causes the nasalization and raising of the diphthong [au].

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
